title: achillea alpina subsp. multiflora
intro: Achillea alpina subsp. multiflora, also known as Alpine Yarrow or multiflowered Alpine Yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever and pain, as well as soothing digestive issues and calming the nervous system. Achillea alpina subsp. multiflora is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
